fields.name: shadow fade distance:world units#NOTE this is only a maximum distance, shadows may fade closer when you exceed the shadow budget, you should balance the total shadows in a scene
midrange detail disappear distance:world units#distance at which the midrange detail disappears
close detail disappear distance:world units#distance at which the close detail disappears
default dialogue#The default dialogue tag for this model (overriden by variants)
default dialogue female#The default FEMALE dialogue tag for this model (overriden by variants)
default dialogue effect#The default dialogue tag for this model (overriden by variants)
Sky parallax percent#If flag checked % between sky pos and camera pos 0=camera
shadow slope scale bias:degrees#controls cutoff point for shadows around edges.  Default is 81 degrees
shadow depth compare bias (dynamic lights)#Default is 0.0008
shadow slope scale bias (dynamic lights):degrees#controls cutoff point for shadows around edges.  Default is 81 degrees
PRT Shadows (soft self-shadow)
fields.definition:
By default, the shadows on each permutation are computed using the first permutation in
every other region as shadow casters.  You can override this behavior below by specifying
which permutation to use as a shadow caster in a given region.

  PRT shadow bounces:
    the number of light bounces to use when computing the global illumination.
    (0 bounces gets only direct light).  Increasing the number of bounces
    increases the calculation time.  1 or 2 bounces should be good enough for
    almost all models.

fields.name: PRT shadow detail!#how much information is recorded about different light directions
PRT shadow bounces#0 means direct light only
Shield impact overrides!
fields.definition: Regular and 1st person shield impact effect overrides

fields.name: instance group#selects an instance group for this variant
region name^:must match region name in render_model
sort order#negative values mean closer to the camera
vitality percentage:[0.1]#percentage of total object vitality
shield material name#set this to make this damage section a shield
minimum stun damage#the minimum damage required to stun this object's health
pre recharge effect warn time:seconds#(main shield only) how long before the recharge begins the pre-recharge effect fires
pre recharge effect marker#(main shield only)
pre recharge abort effect#(main shield only) if the pre-recharge effect is aborted before the actual recharge starts, this effect plays
pre recharge abort effect marker#(main shield only)
overcharge time:seconds#time it takes to reach full "overcharge fraction"
overcharge fraction#fraction to which shields will automatically overcharge, values <= 1.0 are ignored
pre decay time:seconds#time for this section to be active before it will start to decay
decay time:seconds#time for need for this section to fully decay with full health.
health transfering
fields.definition:
In cases of health packs and recharge, this transfers the vitality (an absolute amount) being set to other places.

percent - The heal is transfered, but will not truncate the target's existing vitality amount (it's not healing if things go down).

fields.name: vitality percentage:[0.1]#recharge will stop once this percentage of vitality is reached.
damage threshold#response fires after crossing this threshold.  1=full health
looping effect#will play until the next response is triggered.
destroy instance group#all possible instances from this group will be destroyed
response delay:seconds#time to wait until firing the response. This delay is pre-empted if another timed response for the same section fires.
delay effect#plays while the timer is counting down
skip fraction#0.0 always fires, 1.0 never fires
destroyed child object marker name#when this response fires, any children objects created at the supplied marker name will be destroyed
total damage threshold#scale on total damage section vitality
constraint/group name#can specify a randomly-selected single constraint or the entire group of named constraints
subgroup#if this member is chosen, this subgroup will be chosen as well
instances#instance name, a partial name will choose all matching instances, leave blank for NONE
probability:> 0.0#higher numbers make it more likely
global indirect material name#absorbes AOE or child damage
indirect damage section#absorbes AOE or child damage
minimum stun damage#the minimum damage required to stun this object's health
stun time:seconds#the length of time the health stay stunned (do not recharge) after taking damage
recharge time:seconds#the length of time it would take for the shields to fully recharge after being completely depleted
recharge fraction#0 defaults to 1 - to what maximum level the body health will be allowed to recharge
maximum shield vitality#the default initial and maximum shield vitality of this object
minimum stun damage#the minimum damage required to stun this object's shields
stun time:seconds#the length of time the shields stay stunned (do not recharge) after taking damage
recharge time:seconds#the length of time it would take for the shields to fully recharge after being completely depleted
shield overcharge fraction#fraction to which shields will automatically overcharge, values <= 1.0 are ignored
shield overcharge time#time it takes to reach full "shield overcharge fraction"
damage section flags
fields.definition:
* absorbs body damage: damage to this section does not count against body vitality
* headshottable: takes extra headshot damage when shot
* ignores shields: damage to this section bypasses shields

fields.name: vitality percentage:[0.1]#percentage of total object vitality
Constraint damage type
fields.definition:
* if you specify a constraint group name (see lower section of this block)
  you can specify a constraint damage
* loosening a constraint takes it out of the rigid state - activates it
* destroying a constraint sets the attached body free

fields.name: Constraint destruction
fields.definition:
- a response can destroy a single constraint by naming it explicitly.
- alternatively it can randomly destroy a single constraint from a specified group if the "destroy one group constraint" flag is set
- also it can destroy all constraints in a specified group if the "destroy all group constraints" flag is set

fields.name: Damage response flags
fields.definition:
* kills object: when the response fires the object dies regardless of its current health
* inhibits <x>: from halo 1 - disallows basic behaviors for a unit
* forces drop weapon: from halo 1 - makes the unit drop its current weapon
* kills weapon <x> trigger: destroys the <x> trigger on the unit's current weapon
* destroys object: when the response fires the object is destroyed

fields.name: damage threshold#response fires after crossing this threshold.  1=full health

body damage threshold#response fires after object body damage crosses this threshold, numbers can be negative.  You need to set the flag "body threshold active" for this number to be used. 1=full health
destroy instance group#all possible instances from this group will be destroyed
Response delay
fields.definition: If desired, you can specify a delay until the response fires.This delay is pre-empted if another timed response for the same section fires.The delay effect plays while the timer is counting down

fields.name: destroyed child object marker name
fields.definition: when this response fires, any children objects created at the supplied marker name will be destroyed

fields.name: total damage threshold
fields.definition: scale on total damage section vitality

fields.name: region-state-specific-damage
fields.definition: for fields below, 0.0==inherit from damage seat

fields.name: indirect material name#absorbes AOE or child damage
indirect damage section#absorbes AOE or child damage
shielded state damage section#the model's shielded/unshielded state reflects the depletion of this damage section
marker name^#multiple markers become multiple spheres of the same radius
cone angle#the target is only visible when viewed within this angle of the marker's x axis
damage section#the target is associated with this damage section
variant#the target will only appear with this variant
targeting relevance#higher relevances turn into stronger magnetisms
tracking type#a weapon can track/lock on this target if this string is in the weapon's tracking block
marker name^#multiple markers become multiple spheres of the same radius
cone angle#the target is only visible when viewed within this angle of the marker's x axis
damage section#the target is associated with this damage section
variant#the target will only appear with this variant
targeting relevance#higher relevances turn into stronger magnetisms
SCENARIO LOAD PARAMETERS
fields.definition:
strip-variant <variant-name>
strips a given variant out of the model tag
strip-dialogue
strips all the dialogue for this model i.e. cinematic only

fields.name: shadow cast permutation#leave blank for none

options: starts inactive#this section will be initialized with zero health and will be stunned indefinitely
takes full dmg when object dies
takes full dmg when obj dstryd
headshot#takes extra headshot damage when shot
play recharge effect only when empty
networked (note that primary body and shield are always networked){networked (note that primary body }
can recharge independently#always a shield layer to recharge even if there is an inner shield layer that is stunned
kills object#when the response fires the object dies regardless of its current health
inhibits melee attack#from halo 1 - disallows melee for a unit
inhibits weapon attack#from halo 1 - disallows weapon fire for a unit
inhibits walking#from halo 1 - disallows walking for a unit
forces drop weapon#from halo 1 - makes the unit drop its current weapon
destroys object#when the response fires the object is destroyed
damages weapon primary trigger#destroys the primary trigger on the unit's current weapon
damages weapon secondary trigger#destroys the secondary trigger on the unit's current weapon
can refire#can fire again if the section ever recharges past its threshold
can skip transition effects#will not spawn effects if previous response also fired
destroy one of group#sets the attached body of this constraint free
destroy entire group#sets the attached body of all constraints free
loosen one of group#takes this constraint out of the rigid state - activates it
loosen entire group#takes all constraints out of the rigid state - activates them
ceiling#incoming heal cannot raise the vitality beyond this
parent never takes body damage for us
parent never takes shield damage for us
AI do not damage without player
shield recharge plays only when empty
orphan from postprocess autogen#check this to take control of the new damage info tag block
takes full dmg when object dies
takes full dmg when obj dstryd
takes full dmg when shield depl
kills object#when the response fires the object dies regardless of its current health
inhibits melee attack#from halo 1 - disallows melee for a unit
inhibits weapon attack#from halo 1 - disallows weapon fire for a unit
inhibits walking#from halo 1 - disallows walking for a unit
forces drop weapon#from halo 1 - makes the unit drop its current weapon
destroys object#when the response fires the object is destroyed
damages weapon primary trigger#destroys the primary trigger on the unit's current weapon
damages weapon secondary trigger#destroys the secondary trigger on the unit's current weapon
body threshold active#this resoponse fires when the body healh fraction crosses this boundary
takes damage for children#useful for bipeds shielding/taking damage for weapons
parent never takes damage for us#useful for attached shields
use for network lead vector only
shows tracking reticle#use this model targets center for displaying the targetting reticle
inconsequential target#used in magnetism and campaign saving
model use airprobe lighting first{model use airprobe lighting}

If sky attaches to camera#parallax % between sky pos and camera pos below

no child objects in lightmap shadow
